title: Damped harmonic motion formula
text: The general form for damped harmonic motion is $$f(t)$$ $$=$$ Ae**{-ct}\cos(\omega $$t)$$ or $$f(t)$$ $$=$$ Ae**{-ct}\sin(\omega $$t)$$. $$ \cup se$$ $$cosine$$ $$for$$ $$initial$$ $$displacement$$ $$at$$ $$t=0$$.

title: Identify A and c
text: What are the initial displacement $$A$$ and the damping factor $$c$$ for this scenario?

title: Calculate angular frequency
text: The angular frequency $$\omega$$ is related to the frequency $$f$$ by the formula $$\omega$$ $$=$$ $$2\pi$$ $$f$$. $$This$$ $$value$$ $$is$$ $$the$$ $$same$$ $$as$$ $$the$$ $$previous$$ $$step$$.

title: Calculate \omega
text: Given a frequency of $$0.5$$ cycles per second, what is the angular frequency $$\omega$$?
